title: oxalis bipartita subsp. pabstii
intro: Oxalis bipartita subsp. pabstii, also known as Pabst's wood sorrel, is a plant species belonging to the Oxalis genus, native to Brazil. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its anthraquinone content, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Oxalis bipartita subsp. pabstii include potential uses as a diuretic, to treat constipation, and to aid in weight loss, although further research is necessary to confirm these claims. Oxalis bipartita subsp. pabstii can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often consumed in combination with other herbal remedies.
